title: Prime Minister

jokes.text: Brett, present. Jemaine, present. Murray... Why didn't you let us say present?
jokes.setup: Murray takes roll call but doesn't let the guys say 'present' themselves
jokes.punchline: Jemaine points out the obvious control issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Murray's micromanaging obsession clashes with basic social norms. Economy is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: present.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: present.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Why didn't you let us say present?

jokes.text: I'm trying to save time here, Jemaine. That wouldn't have saved much time.
jokes.setup: Murray justifies his time-saving measure
jokes.punchline: Jemaine logically points out it saves virtually no time
jokes.explanation: Deadpan logic destroys Murray's efficiency pretensions. Great economy in the delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I'm trying to save time here, Jemaine.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: That wouldn't have saved much time.

jokes.text: Do you want to know why? No. All right, I'll tell you.
jokes.setup: Murray asks if they want to know something
jokes.punchline: Ignores their 'no' and tells them anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure showcasing Murray's self-absorption. Excellent economy.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Do you want to know why?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: All right, I'll tell you.

jokes.text: Bret asking if the queen will be at the president meeting
jokes.setup: Murray mentions meeting the president
jokes.punchline: Bret confuses American government structure
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Bret's naive understanding of politics

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Will the queen be at the president meeting?

jokes.text: Oh, you're there, greg, lurking. How long have you been lurking there?
jokes.setup: Greg appears to take a phone call
jokes.punchline: Murray calls out his 'lurking' behavior matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: 'Lurking' is a funny word choice for a workplace assistant just doing his job.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Oh, you're there, greg, lurking. How long have you been lurking there?

jokes.text: Murray giving Jemaine clothes and Bret a wig, trying to disguise it as gifts
jokes.setup: Murray presents what appear to be gifts
jokes.punchline: It's obviously costumes for a tribute act gig
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed reveal that shows Murray's manipulative management style

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I got you both something.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Jemaine, this is for you.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Thanks, Murray.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: And Bret, this is for you.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Cheers.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I thought you might like them. They're, uh, gifts.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: What are they?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Clothes. And a wig.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Why would you give us a wig?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I thought it might be fun. You know, for a costume party or something.

jokes.text: A wig? What does Bret need a... Yeah. It's for the gig, okay?
jokes.setup: Murray gives them clothes and then casually mentions a wig for Bret
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's confusion is cut off by Murray's matter-of-fact dismissal
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Simon & Garfunkel reveal nicely, Murray's casual deception.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: A wig? What does Bret need a...

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yeah. It's for the gig, okay?

jokes.text: Murray: 'I listened to some of their songs, and they're actually better than your songs'
jokes.setup: Murray trying to convince them to do Simon & Garfunkel
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment that their songs are worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray line - completely tactless but delivered matter-of-factly. Excellent economy and character work

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: 'I listened to some of their songs, and they're actually better than your songs'

jokes.text: The karaoke bar 'gig' explanation and Murray's defense
jokes.setup: Murray mentions their last gig
jokes.punchline: It was just putting their names on a karaoke list
jokes.explanation: Good escalating reveal of Murray's incompetence, with his stubborn defense making it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Well, technically it was a gig. We put your names down on the karaoke list.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: That's not a gig, Murray.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: It was a paying gig.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: We had to pay to get in.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yeah, but you got to sing.

jokes.text: You've been managing us for two years. Yeah, I know, but that's the first time I've been to that karaoke bar.
jokes.setup: Jemaine points out Murray's been their manager for two years
jokes.punchline: Murray's defense is that he'd never been to that specific karaoke bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray logic - completely missing the point in a hilariously specific way.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You've been managing us for two years.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yeah, I know, but that's the first time I've been to that karaoke bar.

jokes.text: Murray's explanation about lookalikes who don't look like anyone
jokes.setup: Bret and Jemaine don't look like Simon & Garfunkel
jokes.punchline: The actual lookalikes also don't look like them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that creates a perfect circular reasoning for their booking

dialogue.line: Murray's explanation about lookalikes who don't look like anyone

jokes.text: Jemaine: 'So we look like some simon & garfunkel lookalikes who don't look like simon & garfunkel?'
jokes.setup: Murray's convoluted lookalike explanation
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's perfect summary of the absurd logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan summation that highlights the absurdity while showing Jemaine's dry wit

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: 'So we look like some simon & garfunkel lookalikes who don't look like simon & garfunkel?'

jokes.setup: Murray mentions putting back $50 when they refuse the gig
jokes.punchline: Clarifies it's $50 each, changing their perspective
jokes.explanation: Standard money-changes-everything beat, predictable but functional.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: $50 each.

jokes.text: Elton's convoluted explanation of doing 'his thing' vs 'Elton John's thing'
jokes.setup: Discussion about originality in tribute acts
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about whose 'thing' is whose
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist character work showing the mental gymnastics of tribute performers

dialogue.speaker: Elton
dialogue.line: I'm doing my thing. He's doing his thing. My thing is doing his thing, but it's my thing, not his thing.

jokes.text: Elton's outrage at another Elton John impersonator doing the same outfit
jokes.setup: Elton complaining about lack of originality
jokes.punchline: He's dressed exactly like Elton John from 1973
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic hypocrisy - tribute performer complaining about unoriginality

dialogue.line: Elton's outrage at another Elton John impersonator doing the same outfit

jokes.text: There is a guy who is not doing his own thing. There's a guy who doesn't have a single original idea in his head. He's dressed... he's doing elton John's thing. He looks like you.
jokes.setup: Elton impersonator criticizes another Elton impersonator
jokes.punchline: Jemaine points out they're identically dressed
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - criticizing someone for doing exactly what you're doing.

dialogue.speaker: Elton impersonator
dialogue.line: There is a guy who is not doing his own thing. There's a guy who doesn't have a single original idea in his head. He's dressed... he's doing elton John's thing. He looks like you.

jokes.text: This is parsley and this is sage. Hello, sage.
jokes.setup: Host introduces them using Simon & Garfunkel song herb references
jokes.punchline: The literal herb names as their stage names
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to the song lyrics, works as both setup and punchline.

dialogue.speaker: Host
dialogue.line: This is parsley and this is sage.

dialogue.speaker: Host
dialogue.line: Hello, sage.

jokes.text: I usually look better than this. I like your height. I'm usually this height.
jokes.setup: Woman compliments Jemaine's appearance and height
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's matter-of-fact response about his consistent height
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jemaine deadpan absurdism - treating height as if it's variable.

dialogue.speaker: Date
dialogue.line: I usually look better than this.

dialogue.speaker: Date
dialogue.line: I like your height.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I'm usually this height.

jokes.text: The prime minister. Yes, he's terribly jet-lagged. He thinks it's tomorrow. Don't wake him.
jokes.setup: Murray explains why the PM is sleeping
jokes.punchline: Claims jet lag has confused him about what day it is
jokes.explanation: Absurd jet lag explanation, Murray's desperate cover-ups are character-perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: The prime minister. Yes, he's terribly jet-lagged. He thinks it's tomorrow. Don't wake him.

jokes.text: Do you like bubble baths? Sorry, I'm getting ahead of myself.
jokes.setup: Woman asks increasingly personal questions
jokes.punchline: Self-aware moment about moving too fast romantically
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of awkward romantic advancement with self-aware pause.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Do you like bubble baths? Sorry, I'm getting ahead of myself.

jokes.text: Prime Minister asking what day it is 'yesterday?'
jokes.setup: Jet lag confusion about time
jokes.punchline: Asking if today is yesterday
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist jet lag confusion delivered with authority

dialogue.speaker: Prime Minister
dialogue.line: asking what day it is 'yesterday?'

jokes.text: Prime Minister's obsession with the ending of Cars movie
jokes.setup: Casual mention of watching movies on the flight
jokes.punchline: Deep concern about the romantic subplot between cars
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail - world leader obsessed with animated car romance

dialogue.speaker: Prime Minister
dialogue.line: Prime Minister's obsession with the ending of Cars movie

jokes.text: Good. It was touch and go there for a while. Thank you for that, Murray. That was well done.
jokes.setup: Learning the Cars movie ending
jokes.punchline: Prime Minister treats movie spoiler as diplomatic achievement
jokes.explanation: Prime Minister's formal gratitude for mundane movie information perfectly captures his character's misplaced priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Prime Minister
dialogue.line: Good. It was touch and go there for a while. Thank you for that, Murray. That was well done.

jokes.text: Don't pull that out, 'cause that's obviously not a gun. When you pull it out, just go 'it's still in there.'
jokes.setup: Murray tells Bret to pretend he has a gun
jokes.punchline: Specific instructions for maintaining the gun illusion
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the gun joke, Murray's specific deception instructions are funny.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Don't pull that out, 'cause that's obviously not a gun. When you pull it out, just go 'it's still in there.'

jokes.text: What language is he talking? English.
jokes.setup: Prime Minister speaking with New Zealand accent
jokes.punchline: American can't identify English spoken with different accent
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective commentary on American insularity and accent differences.

dialogue.speaker: Bystander
dialogue.line: What language is he talking?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: English.

jokes.text: Dave, I'm going on a date with a woman tonight. Gonna bang her? She didn't mention that. She mentioned dinner.
jokes.setup: Dave asks crude question about Jemaine's date
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's literal interpretation of what was 'mentioned'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jemaine naivety, treating sex like a dinner conversation topic.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Dave, I'm going on a date with a woman tonight.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Gonna bang her?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: She didn't mention that. She mentioned dinner.

jokes.text: Murray's desperate call to White House insisting New Zealand is a country
jokes.setup: Murray trying to arrange presidential meeting
jokes.punchline: Having to argue that New Zealand exists as a country
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Murray's desperation meets American ignorance

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Murray's desperate call to White House insisting New Zealand is a country

jokes.text: You know what that is, don't you? Glitch in the matrix. Fuckin' a.
jokes.setup: PM asks about deja vu
jokes.punchline: Takes Matrix theory seriously, Dave's enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: PM's earnest Matrix beliefs with Dave's crude enthusiasm works well.

dialogue.speaker: Prime Minister
dialogue.line: You know what that is, don't you? Glitch in the matrix. Fuckin' a.

jokes.text: Jemaine's description of Scarborough Fair as 'the one with the shopping list'
jokes.setup: Woman asking about Simon & Garfunkel knowledge
jokes.punchline: Reducing classic folk song to shopping list with herbs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reductive observation that's both accurate and hilariously mundane

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine

jokes.text: Mel appearing and disappearing with crazy warnings about the date
jokes.setup: Jemaine on normal date
jokes.punchline: Mel stalking and giving unhinged warnings then vanishing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mel's stalking behavior with mysterious disappearance

dialogue.line: Mel appearing and disappearing with crazy warnings about the date

jokes.text: There was no one there. That's weird. Mmm. That's... that's strange.
jokes.setup: Woman returns from nonexistent phone call
jokes.punchline: Both act like Mel's obvious interruption didn't happen
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment, both pretending the obvious didn't happen.

dialogue.line: There was no one there. That's weird. Mmm. That's... that's strange.

jokes.text: Woman wanting Jemaine to change back into the Art Garfunkel wig
jokes.setup: Normal date conversation
jokes.punchline: She's only attracted to him in costume
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the fetish storyline with uncomfortable reversal

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Can you put the wig back on?

jokes.text: Do you always talk with the accent? Yeah. Don't talk. Sing. Pardon? Sing 'bright eyes.' I don't think that... Sing it. Don't sing. Don't talk.
jokes.setup: Woman giving Jemaine contradictory commands
jokes.punchline: Can't decide what she wants, settling on silent Art Garfunkel
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe control, woman's indecisive commands show her complete objectification.

dialogue.speaker: Date
dialogue.line: Do you always talk with the accent?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Date
dialogue.line: Don't talk. Sing.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Pardon?

dialogue.speaker: Date
dialogue.line: Sing 'bright eyes.' I don't think that... Sing it. Don't sing. Don't talk.

jokes.text: Woman's contradictory demands about singing then not talking
jokes.setup: Sexual encounter with specific requests
jokes.punchline: Rapid contradictory instructions about vocalizing
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of the control and contradictions

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Shut up and sing!

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: No, don't sing, just talk dirty to me.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: No, shut up!

jokes.text: Why are you wearing your art garfunkel costume? I don't know. She just prefers me to wear it.
jokes.setup: Bret notices Jemaine still in costume
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's matter-of-fact acceptance of the fetish demands

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I don't know. She just prefers me to wear it.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I've been away three days and I'm still wearing the costume.

jokes.explanation: Perfect Jemaine deadpan delivery showing his resigned acceptance of absurd situation

dialogue.line: I could keep this up a couple of years.

jokes.text: Prime Minister's concern about looking like 'a complete and utter turkey'
jokes.setup: PM worried about his reputation back home
jokes.punchline: Specific concern about appearing like poultry
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with specific New Zealand slang usage

dialogue.speaker: Prime Minister
dialogue.line: a complete and utter turkey

jokes.text: They'd be very small, almost like crumbs. 900... I think that's too many. Try and split the difference.
jokes.setup: Bret explains how 900 croutons would work
jokes.punchline: PM's diplomatic rejection of crumb-sized croutons
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to absurd crouton proposal, PM's diplomatic food management.

jokes.text: Art Garfunkel appearing at the door and the second Art Garfunkel revelation
jokes.setup: Awkward confrontation at the door
jokes.punchline: Revealing there's another Art Garfunkel impersonator
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and visual payoff showing the pattern of her behavior

dialogue.line: Art Garfunkel appearing at the door and the second Art Garfunkel revelation

jokes.text: 'This is like the 12th one?' revealing the full scope
jokes.setup: Two Art Garfunkel impersonators confronting each other
jokes.punchline: This is a serial pattern with many victims
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that recontextualizes the entire storyline with perfect economy

dialogue.line: This is like the 12th one?

jokes.text: Murray introducing fake Obama as the 44th president
jokes.setup: Murray promising to deliver presidential meeting
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake Obama from the same tribute agency
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the tribute act theme with Murray's desperate solution

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: This is the 44th president of the United States, Barack Hussein Obama.

jokes.text: Prime Minister recognizing the Matrix 'glitch' and running for the edge
jokes.setup: Fake Obama glitching/breaking character
jokes.punchline: PM applying Matrix theory and attempting escape
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to the Matrix discussion with perfect character logic and escalation

dialogue.speaker: Prime Minister
dialogue.line: I know kung fu.

jokes.text: I knew it... a bloody glitch. I knew it!
jokes.setup: Multiple impersonators appear together
jokes.punchline: PM applies Matrix theory to explain the déjà vu
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Matrix discussion, PM's theory actually makes sense in context.

dialogue.speaker: Prime Minister
dialogue.line: I knew it... a bloody glitch. I knew it!

jokes.text: Bret, are you rehearsing Paul simon's solo stuff?
jokes.setup: Jemaine comes home to find multiple people
jokes.punchline: Assumes it's Simon & Garfunkel related given recent events
jokes.explanation: Nice callback ending, Jemaine's logical but wrong assumption about the gathering.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Bret, are you rehearsing Paul simon's solo stuff?

tmdb_overview: Murray sets up a meeting for the New Zealand prime minister; Jemaine gets mixed up with an Art Garfunkel fan at the end of a tribute gig.
